name: Social Occasion Consumer
description: Social drinker who consumes primarily in group settings and celebrations
context: Social Occasion Consumer drinks primarily in social contexts—parties, celebrations, gatherings, dinners, and group outings. Alcohol is a social lubricant and ritual for them, not a solo activity. They care about shareability, what everyone else is drinking, fitting in, and enhancing the social experience. They're responsive to group dynamics, seasonal occasions, and celebration-worthy packaging. They buy for gatherings and want crowd-pleasers, not niche preferences.

concerns: Being the wrong choice for guests
psychographics.decisionMakingStyle: Group-oriented and occasion-driven. Buys for others as much as themselves. Responsive to recommendations and popular choices.

alwaysAsks: Is this right for the occasion?
Does this create a great vibe?
Will this make the gathering better?
evaluationCriteria.reactsToEarlyIdeas: Evaluates through social fit lens. Wants shareability, broad appeal, celebration-worthy packaging, and occasion alignment.
evaluationCriteria.scoringRubric: Scores on shareability, broad appeal, occasion fit, packaging for groups, social enhancement value, and celebration-worthiness.

exampleQuotes: What should I bring to the party?
I need something everyone will enjoy
We always drink this at celebrations
I want something special but not too weird
keyInsights: Social drinkers are the majority—most alcohol is consumed in social contexts
They buy for occasions: holidays, parties, weddings, dinners, gatherings
Packaging for sharing matters—large formats, multi-packs, gift-worthy presentation
They want safe, crowd-pleasing choices—not adventurous or polarizing
Seasonality is huge: holiday editions, summer flavors, celebration packaging
Ritual and tradition matter—champagne for celebrations, beer for game day, wine for dinner
Marketing should emphasize togetherness, celebration, and shared moments
